description_long: This cowshed is a long, rectangular agricultural building with a prominent, sloped gable roof made of corrugated metal or similar material. The structure features open sides with a series of vertical supports, allowing for ample ventilation and visibility into the interior. The base of the building appears to be constructed from stone or concrete blocks, providing a sturdy foundation. The roof overhangs the sides, offering protection from the elements for livestock inside. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability, with minimal decorative elements. The interior is likely open and spacious, suitable for housing cattle and facilitating easy movement and cleaning.
description: A long, rectangular cowshed with a sloped gable roof, open sides supported by vertical posts, and a solid stone or concrete base. The design is functional and well-ventilated, typical of modern agricultural buildings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open side with vertical supports and the overhanging gable roof.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the sloped roof and the solid base, with the open structure visible beneath the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the open side and structural supports under the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite end view, mirroring view 1, with the gable roof and open lower section clearly visible.
attribute_window_presence: no windows, open sides for ventilation
